channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Florida sues Target for 'defrauding investors' by selling LGBTQ+ Pride merchandise
item.description: Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier on Thursday announced the state filed a class action lawsuit against Target claiming "the company knowingly misled and defrauded investors by concealing the financial risks of its radical LGBTQ activism.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Kids' disability rights cases stalled as Trump began to overhaul Education Department
item.description: As President Donald Trump reshaped the Education Department, parents say investigations and mediations into kids' disability rights cases ground to a halt.
item.dc:creator: Heather Hollingsworth, Collin Binkley And Annie Ma, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Some New York prison guards charged in beating death of handcuffed inmate appear in court
item.description: UTICA, N.Y. (AP) — Six New York prison guards have been indicted for second-degree murder in the beating death of an inmate who was handcuffed, while four corrections workers were charged with lesser crimes.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL HILL and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Officials investigating bomb threat at Kennedy Center, police say
item.description: The Kennedy Center was evacuated on Thursday after a bomb threat was reported at the venue, according to officials.
item.dc:creator: Odyssey Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Forever 21 may close remaining stores as second bankruptcy looms
item.description: Fashion retailer Forever 21 has begun liquidation sales at several of its locations throughout the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, Fox 5

item.title: Victor Wembanyama expected to miss remainder of season after blood clot diagnosis, Spurs announce
item.description: (AP) -- San Antonio star Victor Wembanyama's season is likely over after he was diagnosed with a blood clot in his right shoulder, a condition that will sideline one of the league's brightest stars and is the second massive health-related hit for the Spurs in a matter of months.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Funeral held for 2 Detroit kids who died in cold while family gets promise of a home
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — A group has stepped forward to provide a home for surviving family members of two Detroit children who died in a cold van in a casino parking garage, an official said. A funeral was held Thursday for Darnell Currie Jr.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A flight to a Toronto paramedics conference becomes a real emergency for a former EMT
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Former paramedic Peter Carlson is still having difficulty putting into perspective the shuddering jolt he and 79 others aboard Delta Air Lines flight 4819 experienced earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bird flu vaccine for chickens given conditional license from USDA
item.description: The company, Zoetis, said this license was granted on the “demonstration of safety, purity, and reasonable expectation of efficacy.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: New arrivals at Guantanamo Bay are Venezuelan immigrants with final deportation orders
item.description: U.S. immigration and military authorities say they have transferred exclusively Venezuelan immigrants who are subject to final deportation orders to the U.S. naval station at Guantanamo Bay, Cuba.
item.dc:creator: Morgan Lee, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Former NFL player Chris Kluwe arrested while protesting MAGA sign at city council meeting
item.description: Former NFL punter Chris Kluwe was detained at a city council meeting in California on Tuesday after delivering an impassioned speech criticizing the design of a plaque to be installed at the Huntington Beach Central Library.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 23% of Americans ended a relationship over money: Survey
item.description: A new report shows nearly 1 in 4 Americans have ended a relationship because they weren't on the same page when it comes to finances.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: Watch live: Trump hosts event honoring Black History Month
item.description: The celebration comes amid the administration's moves to gut federal diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) programs and place DEI employees on leave.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Senate confirms Kash Patel to lead FBI
item.description: The Senate on Thursday confirmed Kash Patel to serve as the head of the FBI, approving a pick with a string of controversial statements who has been accused by Democrats of directing a purge of bureau employees. Patel was confirmed in a 51-49 vote. Two Republicans, Sens.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Trader Joe's recalls frozen item that 'may contain' plastic, removes product from shelves
item.description: A representative for Trader Joe’s told Nexstar on Thursday that the frozen product is not currently available at stores amid the recall.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 2 students arrested for allegedly plotting 'mass casualty attack' at Houston school, FBI says
item.description: Two high school students have been arrested for planning what authorities are calling a “mass casualty attack” at a Houston high school.
item.dc:creator: Chad Washington, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump moves hamper bird flu response as egg prices spike
item.description: When President Trump took office, his administration instituted an external communications blackout across health agencies.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Florida sues Target for 'defrauding investors' by selling controversial LGBTQ+ Pride merchandise
item.description: Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier on Thursday announced the state filed a class action lawsuit against Target claiming "the company knowingly misled and defrauded investors by concealing the financial risks of its radical LGBTQ activism.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 2 people are dead in a small plane collision at a southern Arizona airport
item.description: A midair collision involving two small planes in southern Arizona killed two people Wednesday morning, authorities said. Federal air-safety investigators said each plane had two people aboard when they collided at Marana Regional Airport on the outskirts of Tucson.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE and SEJAL GOVINDARAO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Police arrest man kayaking in Bellagio Fountains during Trump protest
item.description: Because the charges are misdemeanors, a judge released Barnes without him having to post bond, records said.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, NewsNation

item.title: Senate GOP dismayed by Trump, Zelensky war of words
item.description: Public comments from President Trump blaming Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky for Russia’s invasion of his country are unnerving Senate Republicans, who have largely sought to avoid conflicts with the White House.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Creative control of James Bond franchise given to Amazon MGM
item.description: Amazon MGM Studios and longtime Bond custodians Michael G. Wilson and Barbara Broccoli formed a new joint venture in which they will co-own James Bond intellectual property rights — but Amazon MGM will have creative control.
item.dc:creator: Jake Coyle, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Menendez brothers discuss 'bullying and trauma' in prison in rare public remarks, new podcast interview
item.description: Convicted parental killer Erik Menendez discusses the "trauma and bullying" of his life in a California prison in a new interview from behind bars.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Man who admitted to shooting Black teen at wrong door dies before sentencing
item.description: Andrew Lester, a man who pleaded guilty last week to second-degree assault after he shot Ralph Yarl, an unarmed teenage boy in Kansas City in 2023, has died, according to a Clay County prosecutor.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, ABC 4

item.title: Lt. Gov. says Texas Lottery will end if mobile lottery apps aren't banned
item.description: "It is a hard line, couriers have to go," Texas Lt. Gov. Dan Patrick said on Wednesday. The statement comes on the heels of an $83.5 million Lotto Texas jackpot, the fifth-highest in the game's history, which was won through the app Jackpocket -- a DraftKings subsidiary.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Peck, NewsNation

item.title: Black and blue, or white and gold? 'The dress' turns 10 years old
item.description: Ten years ago today, a debate started when a dress was posted on the internet asking what color people were seeing.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The Latest: Judge declines to halt Trump administration's federal workers layoffs
item.description: A federal judge is refusing to temporarily block the Trump administration’s mass layoff of federal workers while a lawsuit brought by five unions moves forward.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump backs idea to send 20% of DOGE savings to American citizens
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump said that he likes the idea of giving some of the savings from Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency back to U.S. citizens as a kind of dividend.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump signs order targeting any federal benefits going to those in the country illegally
item.description: President Trump on Wednesday signed an executive order intended to identify and eliminate any federal financial benefits going to immigrants who entered the country illegally, something already prohibited existing the law.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: New, cheaper iPhone 16e has 'best battery life ever,' Apple says
item.description: "iPhone 16e has the best battery life ever on a 6.1-inch iPhone, lasting up to six hours longer than iPhone 11 and up to 12 hours longer than all generations of iPhone SE," Apple said.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, ABC 4

item.title: IRS plans to lay off 7,000 probationary workers beginning Thursday, AP source says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The IRS will lay off roughly 7,000 workers in Washington and around the country beginning Thursday, a person familiar with the plans told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: This rookie QB card could be worth $50K if you pull it from a new Topps set
item.description: Denver Broncos rookie quarterback Bo Nix impressed on the field, and now a Colorado sports card store is ready to pay $50,000 to keep a unique card featuring him close to home.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Kristensen, Fox 5

item.title: 5 things to watch at this year's CPAC
item.description: Conservatives are gathering in National Harbor, Md., this week for the annual Conservative Political Action Conference (CPAC) in what is poised to be a celebration for the political right following President Trump’s election victory in November.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Coast Guard intercepts 'panga' boat with 20 suspected illegal migrants in waters off San Diego
item.description: Twenty migrants were caught by a U.S. Coast Guard crew that interdicted their boat off the waters of San Diego on Tuesday night, the military branch said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kennedy Center cancels Pride performance featuring Gay Men’s Chorus of Washington
item.description: The Kennedy Center canceled an upcoming performance set to feature the Gay Men’s Chorus of Washington, which the center said was due to financial constraints and scheduling conflicts.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Alabama police department called a 'criminal enterprise' following indictment of 5 officers
item.description: "This is a sad day for law enforcement but a good day for the rule of law," District Attorney Champ Crocker said.
item.dc:creator: Drew Taylor, NewsNation

item.title: U.S. military tests unarmed nuclear missile in Southern California
item.description: If you were outside around 1 a.m. Wednesday on the West Coast, you may have seen or heard the U.S. military test-launching an intercontinental ballistic missile.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5
